understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to move a selectable, pushable object onto specific goal tile(s) marked with a '+', or to otherwise satisfy the success condition defined by the engine (generally, placing the object on the goal).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement could be performed either by directional actions (MOVE_LEFT, MOVE_RIGHT, etc.) or by directly selecting between available pieces with the CLICK action. Only one piece was active at a time. Each MOVE action attempted to move the selected piece in the specified direction, but was blocked by obstacles (such as walls) if the path was not clear, resulting in no position change and still consuming a step. The CLICK action toggled control between selectable pieces, changing the active piece's position to the new coordinates. No movement occurred unless the target cell was selectable (a different controlled object). The player needed to use actions efficiently due to a limited step count per level.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to move a selectable, pushable object onto specific goal tile(s) marked with a '+', or to otherwise satisfy the success condition defined by the engine (generally, placing the object on the goal).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement could be performed either by directional actions (MOVE_LEFT, MOVE_RIGHT, etc.) or by directly selecting between available pieces with the CLICK action. Only one piece was active at a time. Each MOVE action attempted to move the selected piece in the specified direction, but was blocked by obstacles (such as walls) if the path was not clear, resulting in no position change and still consuming a step. The CLICK action toggled control between selectable pieces, changing the active piece's position to the new coordinates. No movement occurred unless the target cell was selectable (a different controlled object). The player needed to use actions efficiently due to a limited step count per level.
